Gartner's 'Companies to Beat' report (Jul 16) named NVDA, AMD, AVGO, MRVL, and Infineon as AI-semiconductor leaders — the cluster has structural demand but tactical entry timing is blocked by extension or weak R:R across most names.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is in pre-earnings posture: GOOG tomorrow, MSFT/META/AMZN Jul 29-30. active_risks: Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz after the ninth night of US airstrikes, halting ~20% of global oil trade through the chokepoint (per Crypto Briefing, Jul 20).
Trump imposed 50% tariffs on Canadian goods (wine, hockey sticks, cement, electric equipment) under Section 338 of the Tariff Act of 1930 — a fresh trade-war front (per CNBC/CNN, Jul 20).
Fed hawkish voices swelling ahead of the Jul 29 FOMC decision; markets price a hold in July but a hike before year-end (per Reuters, Jul 17).
China critical-materials controls: helium export prohibition since Jul 10; Ga/Ge export-ban suspension lapses Nov 27, 2026 — a reinstatement cliff for GaN substrate feedstock.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $82.20 WTI caps the immediate energy-shock damage, but the Hormuz closure is a supply event, not a demand event — every day of disruption tightens physical crude balances and raises the floor. Semis are recovering (SOXX +0.66%) as AI demand signals from TSMC/ASML counter the energy-risk narrative, but the 50% Canada tariffs introduce a fresh inflation impulse that complicates the Fed's July 29 calculus. BigTech earnings season opens tomorrow with GOOG — capex guidance is the next macro catalyst.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for the ninth consecutive night of US airstrikes (per Crypto Briefing, Jul 20). Trump imposed 50% tariffs on Canadian goods under Section 338 (per CNBC, Jul 20).

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds through July; WTI oscillates $80-90; Fed holds Jul 29 but signals a September hike. Semis trade sideways into BigTech earnings — capex guidance comes in at or above consensus, keeping the AI-supercycle thesis intact. NVDA/AMD/AVGO stay in their current ranges.

optimistic.description: US-Iran ceasefire talks begin post-GOOG earnings; WTI drops below $75; Fed signals a pause at Jul 29 on cooling CPI. Semis break above their 50-day averages on capex raises from GOOG/MSFT/META — the AI trade reignites.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz closure persists into August; WTI hits $95+; Canada retaliates against Trump's 50% tariffs. A hyperscaler capex guide-down triggers a semis cluster selloff — META or GOOG 'digestion' framing would break the capex-supercycle thesis across NVDA/AMD/MU/TSM/ASML.

wildcard.description: A US-Iran de-escalation deal is announced within 72 hours — WTI crashes below $70, gold drops below $3,800, and the 'peace dividend' triggers a violent semis/BigTech rally. The Jul 29 FOMC becomes a non-event as the energy premium vanishes.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries until the Hormuz ceasefire or a confirmed capex raise from a major hyperscaler — the energy-shock / tariff / earnings gauntlet is too dense for fresh capital deployment.
